file_insights.purpose: CASL permission middleware implementing RBAC and ABAC access control

patterns_discovered.description: Implements role hierarchy with platform roles (super_admin, admin, sales, support) and organization roles (owner, manager, staff, cashier, viewer)
patterns_discovered.implementation_detail: defineAbilitiesFor function uses switch statement to define abilities based on user.role

patterns_discovered.description: Adds plan-tier based restrictions on top of RBAC using PlanTier hierarchy
patterns_discovered.implementation_detail: getPlanLevel() helper determines feature access based on subscription tier (free, starter, professional, business, enterprise)

patterns_discovered.description: Critical organization-scoped conditions enforced across all tenant roles
patterns_discovered.implementation_detail: orgCondition = { organizationId: orgId } applied to all can() rules for organization roles

patterns_discovered.description: Middleware expects prior execution of authMiddleware and tenantMiddleware to inject user and organization context
patterns_discovered.implementation_detail: permissionMiddleware reads from TenantRequest which is extended by previous middlewares

patterns_discovered.description: Uses CASL's AbilityBuilder to construct ability rules using can/cannot pattern
patterns_discovered.implementation_detail: MongoAbility with [PermissionAction, PermissionSubject] type parameters for flexibility

patterns_discovered.description: Supports field-level permission checks in addition to action-subject level
patterns_discovered.implementation_detail: checkAbility and requirePermission support optional field parameter

gotchas_discovered.description: All organization-scoped rules use organizationId condition. Missing this could cause cross-tenant data leaks
Feature access depends on accurate plan tier from organization.settings. Invalid/null tier defaults to 'free' tier with minimal permissions
permissionMiddleware must execute after tenantMiddleware. Using before tenantMiddleware will fail when attempting to access organization context

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Owner Role Exclusion in Manager Rule
gotchas_discovered.description: Manager role cannot manage other users with owner role: { role: { $ne: 'owner' } }. This is a safety guard but must be documented
Plan restrictions use cannot() which override previous can() rules. Order matters if same action/subject checked multiple times
requirePermission throws generic AppException.forbidden() with action/subject. Users won't know if failure is due to role or plan tier

key_accomplishments: Implemented comprehensive CASL permission middleware with 8 distinct organizational roles
Created plan-tier based feature restrictions (AI, analytics export, branch management)
Established tenant isolation patterns with organizationId conditions
Provided multiple permission check methods (middleware, function-based, field-level)
Defined complete type system for AppAbility, PermissionAction, and PermissionSubject
approach_outcome.architecture_alignment: Aligns with SECURITY_MODEL.md section 3.4 ability builder pattern

recommendations.rationale: Test all role combinations with various plan tiers to catch tenant isolation and permission grant/deny scenarios
Computing abilities on every request could impact performance; consider caching per user session

recommendations.recommendation: Add detailed audit logging for permission checks
recommendations.rationale: Log both successful and failed permission checks with action, subject, role, and organization for security analysis
Provide clear documentation on what permissions each role loses/gains during transitions, especially manager→staff
Create function to explain why a permission was denied (role vs. plan) for better frontend UX

recommendations.recommendation: Validate plan tier at subscription service
recommendations.rationale: Currently defaults to 'free' if planTier is null; add validation to ensure plan tier matches actual subscription
Current implementation doesn't include ownership checks (e.g., can only delete own orders); consider adding this pattern
